reforms.description: The UK government has introduced research and development (R&D) tax credits to support innovation performance, although their cost-effectiveness remains uncertain.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: R&D tax credits incentivize private sector investment in innovation, potentially leading to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The introduction of R&D tax credits has been noted as a key policy to boost innovation performance.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of R&D tax credits represents a significant policy tool aimed at enhancing private sector innovation, although its effectiveness is still under evaluation.
reforms.source_quote: A key policy to boost innovation performance has been the introduction of research and development (R&D) tax credits, although it is uncertain whether these represent a cost-effective use of tax-payer funds.

reforms.description: The UK government plans to enhance publicly-funded adult training programs aimed at the most disadvantaged groups to improve workforce skills.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in adult training for disadvantaged groups is expected to raise skill levels, thereby enhancing productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has plans to address this including through publicly-funded adult training that focuses on the most disadvantaged groups.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative aims to significantly improve the skill levels of a key demographic, which is crucial for enhancing overall workforce productivity.
reforms.source_quote: More broadly, innovation and productivity can be supported by raising the general skill level of the workforce.

reforms.description: The government commissioned the Leitch Review, which highlighted the UK's poor performance in adult learning and set new targets for education levels, including a literacy target of 95% by 2020 and increasing higher education qualifications to 40%.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving adult education and skills is expected to enhance human capital, which is crucial for productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government published its response to the Leitch Review, setting out new targets and initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant overhaul of adult education policy aimed at addressing critical skills gaps in the UK workforce, with long-term implications for economic competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: In July the government published its response, 'World Class Skills', in which it set out new targets across the range of education levels and made a number of announcements including greater employer involvement in the design and direction of training.

reforms.description: In 2000, the United Kingdom introduced a tax relief on R&D expenditure specifically for small firms, which was later extended to large companies in 2002. This reform aimed to incentivize research and development activities within the corporate sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax relief encourages firms to invest in R&D, which can lead to innovation, increased productivity, and ultimately economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax relief was introduced and later extended to large companies.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of R&D tax relief represents a significant policy change aimed at enhancing innovation capacity among firms, particularly small businesses.
reforms.source_quote: In 2000 a tax relief on R&D expenditure was introduced for small firms and later, in 2002, extended to large companies.

reforms.description: The March 2007 Budget announced an increase in the R&D tax credits for small companies from 150% to 175% and for larger firms from 125% to 130%. This reform aims to enhance the incentives for R&D investment among businesses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing the R&D tax credits, the reform encourages firms to invest more in research and development, which can lead to innovation and productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The increase in R&D tax credits was part of the March 2007 Budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment to existing tax incentives aimed at boosting R&D investment, which is crucial for fostering innovation in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: There will be a new environmental tax credit and the rate of R&D tax credits for small companies will rise to 175% (from 150%); for larger firms they will increase from 125% to 130%.

reforms.description: The UK government plans to evaluate the effectiveness of R&D tax incentives before extending their generosity, indicating a commitment to assess and potentially enhance the support for research and development activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Evaluating and potentially enhancing R&D tax incentives can stimulate private sector investment in research, leading to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The United Kingdom plans to do a full evaluation of the scheme as the data becomes available.
reforms.importance_rationale: The evaluation of R&D tax incentives is significant as it aims to improve the effectiveness of a key innovation support mechanism, impacting a wide range of businesses.
reforms.source_quote: In 2006 the United Kingdom commissioned an independent consultancy to conduct a feasibility study into whether an econometric evaluation of the scheme would be possible.

reforms.description: The UK government is reconsidering the balance of direct funding for R&D between SMEs and larger companies, with plans to enhance support for innovation across all sectors through an independent agency.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing support for SMEs, the reform aims to foster innovation and competitiveness in smaller firms, which are crucial for economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Department of Trade and Industry (DTI) Technology Strategy will be administered by an independent agency from July 2007.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how R&D funding is allocated, aiming to better support SMEs and enhance overall innovation capacity in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The government’s main source of direct funding for R&D in both small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) and large companies is the Department of Trade and Industry (DTI) Technology Strategy.

reforms.description: The UK government is promoting university-business collaboration by providing support through the Higher Education Innovation Fund and streamlining governance procedures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing collaboration between universities and businesses can lead to increased innovation, knowledge transfer, and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is providing £110 million a year support for business-university collaboration in England.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant as it aims to strengthen the links between academia and industry, fostering innovation and enhancing the commercialization of research.
reforms.source_quote: The government is providing £110 million a year support for business-university collaboration in England through the Higher Education Innovation Fund.

reforms.description: The UK government announced a package of reforms in the 2007 Budget aimed at enhancing productivity, which includes the introduction of an annual investment allowance and changes to the tax treatment of business investment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms are designed to improve incentives for businesses to invest, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has implemented the reforms as part of the 2007 Budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform package represents a significant shift in tax policy aimed at enhancing business investment, which is crucial for productivity growth.
reforms.source_quote: The 2007 Budget included a package of reforms intended to enhance productivity.

reforms.description: The UK government aims to raise R&D intensity from around 1.7% of GDP in 2004 to 2.5% by 2014 as part of its 10-year plan for science and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D intensity is expected to drive innovation and productivity growth in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has set a target for R&D intensity as part of its long-term strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This ambitious target reflects a significant commitment to enhancing the UK's R&D landscape, which is critical for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government’s 10-year plan for science and innovation aims to raise R&D intensity from around 1.7% of GDP in 2004 to 2.5% by 2014.

reforms.description: The UK government plans to assess the efficiency of fiscal support to R&D, including the R&D tax credit, over the longer term to ensure its effectiveness in promoting research and development activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Evaluating the effectiveness of R&D tax credits can enhance their design, leading to increased R&D investment and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to assess the efficiency of fiscal support to R&D.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant step towards improving the effectiveness of existing R&D tax incentives, which can have a meaningful impact on innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government plans to assess the efficiency of fiscal support to R&D, such as the R&D tax credit, over the longer term.

reforms.description: The 2007 Budget announced increases in the SME R&D tax credit from 150% to 175% and the large company R&D tax credit from 125% to 130%, effective from April 2008.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased tax credits incentivize R&D investment by firms, potentially leading to higher innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax credit increases were announced to take effect in April 2008.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in the fiscal support for R&D, aimed at enhancing the innovation capacity of SMEs and large firms alike.
reforms.source_quote: The 2007 Budget announced increases from April 2008 in some elements of the SME R&D tax credit from 150% to 175% and the large company R&D tax credit from 125% to 130%.
